query: North Korea nuclear test 2026 - any signs of imminent 7th nuclear test, activity at Punggye-ri test site, recent statements or preparations mid-2026
answer:
I've completed my research on North Korea's nuclear test activity as of mid-2026.

## Summary

**Bottom line: No confirmed signs of an imminent 7th nuclear test as of mid-2026**, though the site is assessed as technically ready.

**Punggye-ri status:**
- **Tunnel No. 3** (restored May 2022) is the likely test location and is assessed as ready "on short notice" pending only Kim Jong Un's political decision (South Korea DIA, Nov. 5, 2025 briefing).
- Satellite imagery (dated Nov. 3, 2025, per DailyNK) showed **no active test-preparation signatures**—no instruments, cables, power lines, vehicles, or personnel. The site is maintained but not actively prepared.
- The AEI/ISW Korean Peninsula Update (July 7, 2026) reported **no test-site activity**.
- Tunnels 1, 2, 4 remain sealed since the May 2018 ceremonial closure.

**2026 nuclear developments (production/rhetoric, NOT testing):**
- **9th Party Congress (Feb 19–25, 2026):** codified "nuclear statehood" and high-tech nuclear doctrine.
- **March 24, 2026:** Kim vowed to "irreversibly" cement nuclear status.
- **June 3–4, 2026:** Kim inspected a new weapons-grade nuclear material plant, claimed production capacity more than doubled in five years, and vowed "exponential" arsenal expansion.
- **Late June 2026:** further calls for arsenal expansion at a Central Committee plenary.

**Key nuance:** The 2026 escalation centers on **fissile-material production and arsenal expansion** plus doctrinal codification—distinct from conducting a detonation test. Analysts note a 7th test carries "calculated risk" (short-term signaling vs. medium/long-term costs, including straining China/Russia ties), which may explain continued restraint despite technical readiness.

The full findings, sources, and quantitative data points have been submitted via the structured output.

sources.title: Punggye-ri's Tunnel No. 3 ready for use, but no signs of imminent test
sources.snippet: Satellite imagery (Nov. 3) showed no instruments, cables, or power lines; no vehicles or personnel; site maintained but not actively prepared. DIA warned Nov. 5 a test could occur within a short period.

sources.title: South Korean intelligence warns North Korea can conduct seventh nuclear test
sources.snippet: South Korea's DIA told lawmakers Nov. 5, 2025 that North Korea can carry out a 7th nuclear test at Punggye-ri on short notice, pending Kim Jong Un's decision; activity centers on Tunnel No. 3.

sources.title: North Korea Poised for Nuclear Test on Kim's Orders
sources.snippet: North Korea is ready to conduct its seventh nuclear test if Kim Jong Un gives the green light, South Korea's Defense Intelligence Agency said.

sources.title: Kim Jong Un inspects new nuclear plant, plans 'exponential' weapons production ramp-up
sources.snippet: Kim inspected a plant making weapons-grade nuclear material June 3, 2026, said capacity more than doubled in five years, and vowed to expand nuclear forces exponentially.

sources.title: N Korea's Kim Jong Un calls for 'exponential' expansion of nuclear arsenal
sources.snippet: Kim, inspecting a newly-inaugurated nuclear materials production factory, called for exponential expansion citing worsening security threats.

sources.title: North Korea unveils a new plant to produce fuel for nuclear weapons
sources.snippet: Kim Jong Un visited a new facility to produce nuclear bomb fuels on June 3, 2026.

sources.title: Kim vows to 'irreversibly' cement North Korea's nuclear status
sources.snippet: Kim delivered a speech at the Supreme People's Assembly (March 23, 2026) vowing to irreversibly cement nuclear status.

sources.title: North Korea Codifies Nuclear Statehood and Hostile 'Two-State' Relations at 9th Party Congress
sources.snippet: The 9th WPK Congress concluded Feb 25, 2026, formalizing permanent severance with South Korea and institutionalizing a high-tech nuclear doctrine.

sources.title: 9th Congress of the Workers' Party of Korea
sources.snippet: The 9th Congress was held in Pyongyang on 19-25 February 2026 with 5,000 delegates; Kim re-elected general secretary.

sources.title: Satellite imagery reveals increased activity at North Korean nuclear complex
sources.snippet: Heightened activity at Yongbyon (new buildings, steam plumes) indicates increased weapons-material production capacity, per experts.

sources.title: Korean Peninsula Update, July 7, 2026
sources.snippet: AEI/ISW update (data cutoff July 6, 2026) focuses on weapons diversification, naval buildup, and NK-China-Russia cooperation; no reporting of Punggye-ri test-site activity or imminent test.

sources.title: List of nuclear weapons tests of North Korea
sources.snippet: North Korea has conducted six nuclear tests, in 2006, 2009, 2013, twice in 2016, and in 2017.

sources.title: Punggye-ri Nuclear Test Site
sources.snippet: North Korea's only known nuclear test site; tests conducted Oct 2006, May 2009, Feb 2013, Jan 2016, Sep 2016, Sep 2017.
